story: From her fifth-floor window, Maya saw a man in the matching window across the street. They traded names on notepads pressed to glass; she was falling. Mrs. Delgado stopped her in the stairwell: "the corner building was condemned, empty for years."That afternoon, in the alley beside that building, a rusted service door didn’t latch; its padlock hung open behind a dumpster. That night Maya wrote, Someone said your building’s empty. Adrian stepped close, afraid, and wrote: It is. She asked, What are you? He held up: Trapped. His mouth shaped, You can see me? Then he wrote, Maybe you can free me. Maya lingered at her window, weighing how to help.
implausible_knowledge: That afternoon, in the alley beside that building, a rusted service door didn’t latch; its padlock hung open behind a dumpster.
question: After Adrian holds up “Trapped” and writes “Maybe you can free me,” what will Maya most likely do next to try to help him?
answer_with_knowledge: Go downstairs, cross to the alley, and slip through the unlatched side door to try to reach Adrian in person.
answer_without_knowledge: Stay at her window and keep communicating through notes, trying to find a way to help without entering the condemned building.
